config.detail: Hydra-style config under cfg.model.cfg: same encoder + frontend as the other gigaam-v3 branches; NO `head`, NO `decoding` keys. Top-level `_target_` is `modeling_gigaam.GigaAM` (encoder-only base class), not `GigaAMASR` (which adds head+decoding). model_name=v3_ssl; model_class=ssl. This is the HuBERT-CTC pretrained backbone checkpoint, not an ASR model.

preprocessor.detail: Frontend declared inside config.json under cfg.model.cfg.preprocessor — identical to all sibling branches (n_mels=64, n_fft=320, hop=160, win=320, mel_scale=htk, mel_norm=null, center=false, SpecScaler log).

tokenizer_model.detail: Branch has NO tokenizer.model file. Encoder-only checkpoint emits hidden states; no decoding step.

model_weights.detail: PyTorch pickle, 441,613,947 bytes (~441 MB). ~110M params at F32 — same encoder size as the other branches; the head-related delta is sub-1 MB, so encoder dominates the file size.

safetensors_metadata.detail: Repo ships PyTorch pickle, not safetensors.

model_card.detail: Model card describes v3_ssl as the HuBERT-CTC pretraining checkpoint (700k hours Russian speech). No WER numbers — by definition the SSL checkpoint does not transcribe.

reference_modeling_code.detail: Returns a `GigaAM` instance (encoder + frontend only) — no `.transcribe()` method. Users get encoder hidden states via the forward pass and fine-tune their own downstream heads.

dtype.evidence: pytorch_model.bin is 441 MB ≈ 110M params at F32. salute-developers/GigaAM PyTorch checkpoints are stored at F32.

reference_rationale: Same as the rest of the gigaam family: the `gigaam` PyPI package (MIT) is the canonical loader. `gigaam.load_model('v3_ssl')` returns a `GigaAM` (encoder + frontend) instance with no `.transcribe()` method; the contract is the forward pass producing encoder hidden states.
known_risks: ENCODER-ONLY CHECKPOINT, NOT AN ASR MODEL. There is no decoding step, no tokenizer, no transcribe() method. Schema's architecture_pattern is set to `encoder-ctc` because that is the upstream HuBERT-CTC pretraining objective — but at inference time this variant emits hidden states only.

transcribe.cpp is an ASR runtime; supporting an encoder-only checkpoint requires a NEW exposure path (e.g. a `--emit-encoder-output` CLI flag, or a separate `transcribe-feature` CLI). Without that, the SSL variant has no user-observable capability inside the runtime. This is the largest open design question for porting this variant.

Encoder + frontend tensors are expected IDENTICAL to gigaam-v3-e2e-rnnt's encoder pre-finetune state, but may differ post-finetune (the rnnt/ctc/e2e_* branches are fine-tuned from the SSL checkpoint, so encoder weights drift). Stage 3 must compare encoder tensors across all five branches to identify which (if any) share weights. The SSL checkpoint is the common ancestor.

Stage 7 (WER acceptance) is NOT APPLICABLE for this variant. The acceptance gate is encoder-output numerical match at Stage 4: tensor-by-tensor compare against `gigaam.load_model('v3_ssl').encoder(features)` output. No FLEURS run, no upstream WER, no per-quant WER table at Stage 7.
Rotary positional embeddings inside Conformer attention — same encoder code path as siblings.
Conv1d subsampling, SpecScaler log, center=false, mel_scale=htk, mel_norm=null — same frontend gotchas.

Capabilities object is filled with all-negative / empty values because the schema's `languages` array semantics assume a transcribing model; this variant transcribes nothing. The intake degrades capabilities to empty rather than fabricating false advertising.

intake_gaps.reason: Set to `encoder-ctc` to match the upstream HuBERT-CTC pretraining objective, but the runtime exposure is encoder-output-only. Schema enum has no `encoder-only` option. Acceptable as Stage-4-level encoder-tensor comparison; Stage 7 (WER) does not apply.

transcribe-cli has no encoder-output emission mode today. Porting this variant to user-observable capability requires a new CLI/API path; until then the variant is loadable but not directly useful at runtime.
No upstream WER for an encoder-only pretraining checkpoint. Validation is encoder-tensor numerical parity, not WER.
